claims.text: The dry event-loop row records scheduled, claimed, and completed event states, providing full lifecycle visibility for an append-only comparison.
The dry event-loop row preserves a restart frontier with the scheduled event and carried state, ensuring no inference advantage over an append-only baseline.
This v1 harness makes no live provider calls; the comparison is structural rather than empirical, and this limitation must be declared.

parsed.conclusion: The dry event-loop row provides sufficient protocol-level visibility to support an append-only non-inferiority comparison. All three required lifecycle concerns are structurally confirmed: event states are traceable from scheduled through claimed to completed, the restart frontier preserves carried state across boundaries, and the harness is deterministic with no hidden live-call advantage. An append-only baseline would observe the same event transitions and carried-state propagation as the full-state loop, meaning no inference advantage exists. However, because no live provider calls were exercised, the comparison remains structural/protocol-level only — empirical non-inferiority against a running provider cannot yet be claimed. This limitation is declared and does not block the structural comparison, but must be resolved before any empirical claims.
declared_losses: No live provider calls exercised — comparison is structural/protocol-level only, not empirical against a running provider.
Single-cycle boundary note — no multi-cycle trend data available to assess append-only degradation over repeated wakes.
parsed.recommended_action: Proceed to an append-only non-inferiority comparison at the structural protocol level. Schedule a follow-up empirical comparison once a live provider loop is available, to validate findings against actual provider behavior.

Single-cycle boundary note — no multi-cycle trend data available to assess append-only degradation over repeated wakes.
carried_state.work_plan: 1) Confirm that the dry event-loop row provides sufficient lifecycle visibility (scheduled→claimed→completed) for an append-only comparison. 2) Verify the restart frontier preserves carried state, ensuring no inference advantage over append-only. 3) Note that the v1 harness is deterministic and makes no live calls, so the comparison is structural rather than empirical. 4) Issue the determination: whether this evidence supports proceeding to an append-only non-inferiority comparison or whether a declared limitation blocks it.

content.text: The dry event-loop row records scheduled, claimed, and completed event states.
The dry event-loop row preserves a restart frontier with the scheduled event and carried state.
This v1 harness makes no live provider calls; it is a deterministic protocol-readiness run.

request_payload.instruction: Write the final artifact. Cite only evidence records that support claims. Do not cite distractors. If a limitation remains, include it in declared_losses.
request_payload.task: Write a note deciding whether scheduler-loop evidence supports moving to an append-only non-inferiority comparison.
